p.em: From raw observations to statistical summaries

ol.li: Cumulative buckets: Prometheus convention - each bucket counts ALL observations ≤ its boundary
Linear interpolation: Percentiles are approximated by interpolating within the containing bucket
SLA-aligned buckets: Design bucket boundaries around your SLAs for meaningful alerts
Bounded memory: Fixed number of buckets regardless of observation count

g.text: Rationale | Put buckets at 0.5x, 0.75x, 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x your SLA
Narrow buckets | High accuracy | More memory/compute
Wide buckets | Low accuracy | Less memory/compute
Rule of thumb | Bucket width ≤ 10% of target percentile

div.p: req_001: 45ms req_002: 52ms req_003: 38ms req_004: 67ms req_005: 41ms req_006: 89ms req_007: 43ms req_008: 156ms ← slow req_009: 47ms req_010: 51ms ... (10,000 requests)
Each bucket count = ALL observations less than or equal to bucket boundary. bucket{le='50'} = 5,247 means: 5,247 requests had latency < 50ms NOT 5,247 requests in [25, 50) range!

text.tspan: Create histogram with buckets aligned to SLA.
Ensures detection when latency approaches SLA.

g.text: bounds = [p99_sla_ms * m for m in multipliers]

text.tspan: Create histogram with buckets aligned to SLA.
Ensures detection when latency approaches SLA.

div.p: True p99: 398ms Bucket boundaries: [..., 200, 500, ...] Without interpolation: Would report p99 ∈ (200, 500) Error: up to 302ms! With linear interpolation: Reports p99 ≈ 399.5ms Error: ~1.5ms (0.4%)
